name: Stocktwits
description: Twitter-shaped social network for traders, organized by ticker cashtags ($AAPL, $TSLA, $NVDA). Provides per-ticker message streams + a bullish/bearish sentiment label many posters self-tag with — useful for gauging retail mood, mention-volume spikes (often a contrarian signal at extremes), and discovering what tickers are getting unusual attention. Reads work without auth at limited rate; supply an access_token to raise limits. Get one at https://stocktwits.com/developers/docs.

credential_fields.name: access_token
credential_fields.label: Access Token (optional)
credential_fields.description: OAuth access_token from https://stocktwits.com/developers/apps. Optional — public reads work without it, but rate limits are much tighter (200/hour without token vs 400+/hour with).

tools.name: symbol_stream
tools.description: Recent messages tagged with one ticker. Each message includes id, body, sentiment ('Bullish' / 'Bearish' / null), user (with follower count + ideas count + watchlist count), created_at, and any links. Use to read the retail crowd's take on a name and to count bullish-vs-bearish posts as a rough sentiment ratio.

symbol.description: Ticker without $ prefix — AAPL, TSLA, BTC.X (crypto).

since.description: Return messages with id > this (for incremental polling).

max.description: Return messages with id <= this (for paging backwards).

tools.name: trending_symbols
tools.description: Top trending tickers across Stocktwits right now — the names retail is buzzing about. Useful for discovery of where unusual attention is concentrating; surge of trending = often precedes price movement (any direction).

tools.name: trending_stream
tools.description: Messages flowing through the platform's overall 'trending' stream — like trending_symbols but as message-level granularity. Use for general retail sentiment headlines.

tools.name: search_symbols
tools.description: Find tickers matching a query — useful when an agent has a company name but not a ticker.

tools.name: user_stream
tools.description: Messages from a specific Stocktwits user — useful for following known smart-money or contrarian-indicator accounts.

tools.name: watchlist_stream
tools.description: Messages mentioning any symbol in a public watchlist. Useful for thematic monitoring (e.g. a watchlist of AI semiconductors → one query returns the chatter across all of them).
